title: TR-MRV-Bench: a public per-facility emissions benchmark for Turkish CBAM-scope industry
description:
TR-MRV-Bench is a public per-facility CO₂ emissions benchmark for Turkish CBAM-scope industry (cement, steel, aluminum, fertilizer). It comprises 59 facilities (34 cement, 16 steel, 6 fertilizer, 3 aluminum), with 21 audit-grade Scope 1 strong labels from operator Integrated Annual Reports, sustainability statements (GRI for pre-2024 disclosures; TSRS-compliant for CY 2024 onwards per the KGK mandate effective 1 January 2024), and ISO 14064-1 audit reports issued by TÜRKAK-accredited verifiers.

The bench is released alongside a closed-form physics baseline — tCO₂ = capacity × route-EF × capacity-factor — that reduces pooled per-plant log-MAE by 85.3% versus the EU CBAM default in leave-one-disclosure-out cross-validation. The result is triple-corroborated: empirical fit on n=21 TR plants, external validity on 789 EU plants × 10,691 verified facility-years from the EU Transaction Log (Verifier B6), and independent algorithmic rediscovery by PySR symbolic regression (Verifier B7) which converges to (log_cap + log_ef) + log_cf at complexity 5 with bit-identical MSE to the hand-crafted formula.

The bench was the empirical basis for a formal submission to the European Commission's CBAM default-values implementing act consultation (May/June 2026), proposing audit-anchored Turkey-specific defaults per CBAM route in place of the 90th-percentile-of-global anchor.

All code, data, and verifier outputs are released under Apache-2.0. Live site: iz-b0n.pages.dev. Source: github.com/abgnydn/iz.
notes: Issued in support of TR-MRV-Bench v0.3 — first Zenodo-DOI release after triple-corroboration verifiers (B6 EU-EUTL external validation; B7 PySR symbolic-regression rediscovery) and the formal EU CBAM consultation submission. Apache-2.0 license; data, code, and paper all bundled.
